title: salix aurita f. spathulata
intro: Salix aurita f. spathulata, also known as the largeleaf willow or northern willow, is a subspecies of the Salix aurita plant, which is a type of willow tree native to Europe and northern Asia. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of Salix aurita f. spathulata include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. Salix aurita f. spathulata can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including salves, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being.
